title: phaseolus umbellatus
intro: Phaseolus umbellatus, also known as the sand wattle bean or the purple flowered bean, is a perennial legume native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its purple flowers and distinctive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phaseolus umbellatus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and its seeds can be roasted and used as a coffee substitute, while its leaves and flowers can be used in soups and salads.
